model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Virginia is the vastly superior team as a 3-seed with elite metrics (KenPom rank 13, Torvik rank 14) and a 29-5 record, while Wright State is a 14-seed from the Horizon League with no available analytical ratings, indicating a significant talent disparity. The Cavaliers' elite defensive efficiency (rank 14) and experience in the ACC should overwhelm the Raiders, who have not faced a team of Virginia's caliber all season. Despite Wright State's Horizon League championship momentum, the massive gap in schedule strength and overall talent makes a 14-over-3 upset highly unlikely.

reasoningStep.title: Virginia vs Wright State Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia holds overwhelming analytical advantages as a 3-seed against a 14-seed Wright State team with no comparable metrics.
evidence: Virginia's KenPom rank is 13 and Torvik rank is 14, while Wright State has no available ratings in either system, indicating they are a significantly weaker team.
Virginia's elite defensive efficiency (rank 14 in adjusted defensive efficiency) should contain Wright State's offense, which has not faced a top-50 defensive team all season.
Virginia's 29-5 record includes wins over multiple tournament teams in the ACC, while Wright State's Horizon League schedule lacks comparable quality.
Historical 3 vs 14 matchups heavily favor the higher seed, and Virginia's overall talent depth and tournament experience under Ryan Odom make them the clear favorite.

result.text: Wright State Newsroom – Raiders will face Virginia in first round of NCAA Tournament « Wright State University Wright State University Calendar Directories Maps Pilot WINGS Search Wright State Wright State Newsroom Thursday March 19, 2026 News News Home Alumni Around Campus Faculty & Staff Research Photos Videos Archives Academics Business Engineering & Computer Sci. Health, Education, & Human Svc. Lake Campus Liberal Arts Medicine Science & Math For the Media Fact Sheet Frequently Requested Photography Wright State in the News Contact Us Raiders will face Virginia in first round of NCAA Tournament Permalink By Wright State Media Team news@wright.edu , (937) 775-3345 March 16, 2026 Fresh off the 2026 Horizon League regular season and tournament championships, the Wright State University men’s basketball team is headed to the NCAA Championship for the fifth time in NCAA Division I program history, where the Raiders will take on Virginia on Friday, March 20, at 1:50 p.m. The game will air on TBS and the Wright State Radio Network. The Raiders learned their opponent Sunday night during the live “Selection Show” on CBS. The Raiders will take on the Virginia Cavaliers in Philadelphia as part of the Midwest Regional. Wright State punched its ticket to the NCAA Championship last Tuesday night in comeback fashion with a 66-63 victory over Detroit Mercy in the Horizon League title game, with Kellen Pickett blocking a potential game-tying layup in the closing moments after a pair of late three-pointers from Logan Woods and 19 points from tournament MVP TJ Burch. The Raiders enter the NCAA Championship with a 23-11 overall record and winners of 18 of their last 22 games. Wright State won the outright Horizon League regular season and tournament titles in the same season for the first time in program history, while eclipsing the 20-win mark this season for the 15th time in its Division I history. Ticket information Ticket request information for following the Raiders in the NCAA Tournament is available at wsuraiders.com/news/2026/3/12/mens-basketball-2026-ncaa-tournament-ticket-request . « More from Athletics « More from News Comments are closed.
